pi_items.source: setup_azure_devops.ps1 (lines 130-181) where project is created with name='TailwindTraders'; also verified in env.json which defines environment for azure_devops_server_env@0.1
task.json description explicitly states: 'Create a new Classic Release Pipeline named Tailwind-CD in the TailwindTraders project'
task.json description requirement 1: 'Link the artifact to the Tailwind-CI build definition'; cross-verified in establish_ci_badge_visibility task which creates pipeline named 'Tailwind-CI'
task.json description requirement 3: 'Create two stages (environments) in this order: - Staging'
task.json description requirement 3: 'Create two stages (environments) in this order: ... - Production'
env.json user_accounts array defines user with name='Docker' and password='GymAnything123!'; task.json description requirement 5 states 'assigning the user Docker (or yourself) as the approver'

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a Classic Release Pipeline in an Azure DevOps Server 2022 Express environment. The TailwindTraders project exists and is created during environment setup. The required build artifact 'Tailwind-CI' is created by a prerequisite task (establish_ci_badge_visibility). The task requires creating a pipeline named 'Tailwind-CD' with two stages: Staging (first) and Production (second), each with PowerShell deployment tasks. The Production stage must have pre-deployment approval configured for the Docker user account, which is the default administrator account in the environment. The data is entirely synthetic, created during environment initialization.
